outdatedCompileServer.title: Compile server update required
outdatedCompileServer.description: Compile aborted because your local compile-server image is out of date. Please update to the latest image and try again.
outdatedCompileServer.stepsHeading: Please run the following 3 steps in Docker Desktop
outdatedCompileServer.step1Body: On the Images tab, click the ︙ menu next to digicollc/digicode-compile-server and choose Pull.
outdatedCompileServer.step2Body: On the Containers tab, Stop the running container, then Delete it. Stop alone leaves the green indicator on the old image in the Images tab, so Delete is required as well.
outdatedCompileServer.step2Hint: You must click both Stop and Delete.
outdatedCompileServer.step3Body: On the Images tab, click Run ▶ next to digicollc/digicode-compile-server:latest. In Optional settings, set Host port and Container port both to 3001.
outdatedCompileServer.retryNote: After updating, press the compile button again. The version check will rerun automatically.

i2c.scanTooltip: Scan for connected I2C devices and print addresses to Serial
i2c.writeTooltip: Write a byte to an I2C device
i2c.readTooltip: Read a byte from an I2C device
i2c.writeRegTooltip: Write a value to a specific register of an I2C device
i2c.readRegTooltip: Read a value from a specific register of an I2C device

spi.beginTooltip: Initialize SPI communication with chip select pin
spi.transferTooltip: Send a byte via SPI and receive a byte back

interrupt.attachTooltip: Attach an interrupt handler to a pin. Handler runs when the pin state changes. Avoid delay() and Serial inside the handler.
interrupt.detachTooltip: Remove the interrupt handler from a pin
interrupt.checkTooltip: Check and execute the interrupt handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.

cam.initTooltip: Initialize camera. Select board model — pins are auto-configured. Returns true if successful. Call in setup block.
cam.captureTooltip: Take a photo and store in internal buffer. Returns true if successful. Call camera_save_sd or camera_send_http next.
cam.saveSdTooltip: Save captured image to SD card as JPEG. Call camera_capture first. Returns true if saved. Requires SD initialized (sd_begin).
cam.sendHttpTooltip: Send captured image via HTTP POST. Call camera_capture first. WiFi required. Returns true if sent.
cam.streamTooltip: Start MJPEG streaming server on specified port. WiFi required. View in browser: http://<ESP32-IP>:<port>/stream

can.initTooltip: Initialize CAN bus with TX/RX pins and baud rate. A CAN transceiver IC (e.g. SN65HVD230) is required between ESP32 and CAN bus. Returns true if successful.
can.sendTooltip: Send a CAN message with specified ID and data string (max 8 bytes). ID is an integer (e.g. 0x123 = 291).
can.availableTooltip: Returns true if a new CAN message was received. After true, use can_get_id and can_get_data. Place in loop block.
can.getIdTooltip: Get the ID of the last received CAN message. Call after can_receive_available returns true.
can.getDataTooltip: Get the data of the last received CAN message as a String. Call after can_receive_available returns true.

ir.receiverInitTooltip: Initialize IR receiver on the specified pin. Call in setup block.
ir.receiverDecodeTooltip: Read received IR code as hex string. Returns "0" if no signal. Place in loop block and compare to known codes.
ir.sendTooltip: Send an IR signal with the specified protocol and code. Use code captured by ir_receiver_decode.
ir.senderinit_tooltip: Initialize IR sender LED on the specified pin. Call in setup block.

tft.initTooltip: Initialize TFT display. Select driver (ILI9341/ST7789/ST7735) and set CS/DC/RST pins. Uses hardware SPI.
tft.fillScreenTooltip: Fill the entire screen with one color.
tft.pixelTooltip: Draw a single pixel at (X, Y).
tft.rectTooltip: Draw a rectangle. Uncheck fill for outline only, check fill for solid rectangle.
tft.circleTooltip: Draw a circle centered at (X,Y) with radius R. Check fill for solid circle.
tft.setCursorTooltip: Set text cursor position, size and color. Call before tft_print. Size 1=6x8px, 2=12x16px etc.
tft.printTooltip: Print text at the current cursor position. Call tft_set_cursor first.
tft.colorRgbTooltip: Convert RGB values (0-255 each) to 16-bit RGB565 color code for TFT drawing blocks.

rfid.initM5Tooltip: Initialize M5Stack RFID 2 Unit (WS1850S) via I2C. Address 0x28, no CS/RST needed. This device is certified for use in Japan (技適).
rfid.isPresentTooltip: Returns true if an RFID card/tag is detected. Place in loop block.
rfid.readUidTooltip: Read the card UID as hex string (e.g. "04:A3:B5:C9"). Call after rfid_is_card_present returns true.
rfid.readDataTooltip: Read 16 bytes from the specified block (0-63). Uses default auth key (0xFF×6). Avoid block 0.
rfid.writeDataTooltip: Write up to 16 chars to specified block. Returns true on success. Avoid block 0 and sector trailers (3,7,11...).

ws.connectTooltip: Connect to a WebSocket server. Returns true if successful. Requires WiFi connection.
ws.sendTooltip: Send a text message to the connected WebSocket server.
ws.onMessageTooltip: Execute handler when a WebSocket message is received. Use wsMessage variable. Place in loop block.
ws.isConnectedTooltip: Returns true if currently connected to a WebSocket server.

server.startTooltip: Start ESP32 as a WebSocket server. HTTP serves the controller HTML, WS handles channel traffic. Place in setup after WiFi is connected.
server.registerTooltip: Declare a channel for the controller UI. The widget type is inferred from id / label / data type / min-max / read-write-notify. Place in setup.
server.onMessageTooltip: Run the handler when a write to the given channel is received. Use "WebSocket Received" inside HANDLER to read the value. Placement-independent (loop or setup).
server.sendTooltip: Broadcast the value on the given channel to every connected controller as a JSON envelope.
server.onConnectTooltip: Run the handler when a new controller connects. E.g. broadcast the current state right after connection.
server.clientCountTooltip: Returns the number of currently-connected controllers.
server.receivedValueTooltip: Inside a WebSocket on-message handler, returns the last received value as a string. Returns empty string outside the handler.

uart2.beginTooltip: Initialize Serial2 (UART2) with specified baud rate and GPIO pins. Default: RX=16, TX=17.
uart2.printTooltip: Send data via Serial2 without newline.
uart2.printlnTooltip: Send data via Serial2 with newline at the end.
uart2.readTooltip: Read one byte from Serial2. Returns -1 if no data available.
uart2.readUntilTooltip: Read from Serial2 until the terminator character. Returns the received string.
uart2.availableTooltip: Returns the number of bytes available to read from Serial2.

lcd.initTooltip: Initialize LCD via I2C. Common addresses: 0x27 (PCF8574) or 0x3F (PCF8574A). Use I2C scanner to find your address.
lcd.printTooltip: Print text at the current cursor position on the LCD.
lcd.printAtTooltip: Move cursor to (col, row) and print text. Col 0 = leftmost, Row 0 = top row.
lcd.clearTooltip: Clear all text on the LCD and move cursor to home position (0, 0).
lcd.backlightTooltip: Turn the LCD backlight on or off.

vl53l0x.initTooltip: Initialize VL53L0X ToF distance sensor via I2C (address 0x29). Returns true if found. Requires Adafruit VL53L0X library.
vl53l0x.distanceTooltip: Read distance in mm from VL53L0X. Returns 0 if out of range or error. Effective range: 30-1200mm.

as5600.initTooltip: Initialize AS5600 magnetic encoder via I2C (address 0x36). Returns true if magnet detected. Requires Seeed_Arduino_AS5600 library.
as5600.angleTooltip: Read absolute angle (0-360°) from AS5600. 12-bit resolution (0.088°/step).
as5600.rawTooltip: Read raw 12-bit value (0-4095) from AS5600. Full rotation = 4096 steps.

mpu6050.initTooltip: Initialize MPU6050 sensor via I2C. Requires Adafruit MPU6050 library.
mpu6050.updateTooltip: Read sensor values and update pitch/roll with complementary filter. Call in loop block.
mpu6050.accelTooltip: Get acceleration on specified axis (m/s²). Call mpu6050_update first.
mpu6050.gyroTooltip: Get angular velocity on specified axis (rad/s). Call mpu6050_update first.
mpu6050.tempTooltip: Get MPU6050 internal temperature (°C). Note: chip temperature, not ambient.
mpu6050.angleTooltip: Get pitch or roll angle (degrees) using complementary filter. Call mpu6050_update in loop.
mpu6050.calibrateTooltip: Calibrate while stationary. Offsets stored in RAM (mpu_offset_*). Save to NVS with Preferences blocks if needed.

bme280.initTooltip: Initialize BME280 via I2C. Default address 0x76, use 0x77 if SDO pin is HIGH.
bme280.readTooltip: Read temperature (°C), humidity (%), or pressure (hPa) from BME280.

bmp280.initTooltip: Initialize BMP280 via I2C. Note: BMP280 has no humidity sensor.
bmp280.readTooltip: Read temperature (°C) or pressure (hPa) from BMP280.
bmp280.altitudeTooltip: Calculate altitude (m) from current vs sea level pressure. Standard: 1013.25 hPa.

battery.voltageTooltip: Read battery voltage via ADC with resistor divider. Set ratio (e.g. 2.0 for equal resistors). Returns voltage in V.
battery.percentageTooltip: Convert battery voltage to percentage (0-100%). Set min/max for your battery type (Li-ion: 3.0-4.2V).

ble.uartSetuopTooltip: Initialize BLE and start Nordic UART Service (NUS). Use nRF Connect app to connect and communicate.
ble.uartWriteTooltip: Send a string to the connected smartphone via BLE Notify (NUS TX characteristic).
ble.uartOnReceiveTooltip: Execute handler when a BLE UART message is received. Use the BLE Received block inside the handler to read the received text. Place in loop block.
ble.receivedValueTooltip: Get the message received last via BLE. Use only inside a BLE On Receive or BLE On Write handler. Returns an empty string when no message has arrived.
ble.isConnectedTooltip: Returns true if a smartphone is currently connected via BLE.
ble.disconnectTooltip: Disconnect the currently connected BLE device and restart advertising.
ble.beaconTooltip: Start broadcasting as an iBeacon with the specified UUID, major and minor values. Detectable by smartphone apps.
ble.scanStartTooltip: Start scanning for nearby BLE devices for the specified duration. Use ble_on_device_found to handle results.
ble.onDeviceFoundTooltip: Execute handler when a BLE device is found during scan. Inside the handler, use the BLE Scan Name / Address / RSSI value blocks to read the detected values.
ble.scanFoundNameTooltip: Get the name of the BLE device just detected. Use only inside a BLE On Device Found handler. Returns an empty string when no device has been detected.
ble.scanFoundAddressTooltip: Get the MAC address of the BLE device just detected. Use only inside a BLE On Device Found handler. Returns an empty string when no device has been detected.
ble.scanFoundRssiTooltip: Get the RSSI (dBm; negative, closer to 0 = stronger signal) of the BLE device just detected. Use only inside a BLE On Device Found handler. Returns 0 when no device has been detected.
ble.getRssiTooltip: Get the RSSI (signal strength) of the connected BLE device in dBm (negative, closer to 0 = stronger signal).
ble.initTooltip: Initialize BLE stack. Call before ble_add_service. Use ble_uart_setup instead if only using Nordic UART.
ble.addServiceTooltip: Create a custom GATT service with the given UUID. Add characteristics next, then call ble_start_advertising.
ble.label: label
ble.addCharTooltip: Add a characteristic to a GATT service. The label / type / min / max fields are used by the auto-generated WebBLE controller UI (min/max apply to the slider widget when type is numeric). Check read/write/notify as needed.
ble.notifyTooltip: Send a notify to the connected device via the specified characteristic UUID. Notify must be enabled on the characteristic.
ble.onWriteTooltip: Execute handler when the specified characteristic is written by the connected device. Use the BLE Received block inside the handler to read the received text. Place in loop block.
ble.startAdvTooltip: Start BLE advertising after all services and characteristics are added. Smartphones can then discover and connect to this device.

ntp.syncTooltip: Synchronize time from NTP server. Timezone offset in seconds (JST=32400, UTC=0). Requires WiFi connection.
ntp.unixtimeTooltip: Get current Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01). Returns 0 if not synchronized.
ntp.formattedTooltip: Get current time as a formatted string. Format: %Y=year, %m=month, %d=day, %H=hour, %M=minute, %S=second.
ntp.componentTooltip: Get a specific component of the current time (year, month, day, hour, minute, or second).

rtc.initTooltip: Initialize RTC module (DS3231 or DS1307) via I2C. Returns true if found. Requires RTClib library.
rtc.setTimeTooltip: Set the RTC date and time.
rtc.getComponentTooltip: Get a time component from the RTC module.
rtc.formattedTooltip: Get RTC time as "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" formatted string.

preferences.beginTooltip: Open a Preferences namespace for read/write (ESP32 NVS flash storage).
preferences.endTooltip: Close the Preferences namespace and free resources.
preferences.putTooltip: Save a value (int/float/string) to Preferences storage with the given key.
preferences.getTooltip: Read a value from Preferences storage. Returns the default value if the key does not exist.
preferences.removeTooltip: Delete a specific key from Preferences storage.
preferences.clearTooltip: Delete all keys in the current Preferences namespace.

eeprom.writeTooltip: Write a byte value to EEPROM at the specified address (0-511). EEPROM.commit() is called automatically.
eeprom.readTooltip: Read a byte value from EEPROM at the specified address (0-511).

sd.beginTooltip: Initialize SD card with the specified CS pin. Returns true if successful.
sd.writeTooltip: Write content to a file on SD card. Append adds to existing file, Overwrite replaces it.
sd.readTooltip: Read all contents of a file from SD card as a String.
sd.existsTooltip: Check if a file exists on SD card. Returns true if found.
sd.deleteTooltip: Delete a file from SD card.
sd.csvTooltip: Append a 3-column CSV row to a file on SD card. Ideal for data logging.

fs.mountTooltip: Mount the LittleFS filesystem. Returns true if successful.
fs.writeTooltip: Write content to a file in LittleFS (internal flash storage).
fs.readTooltip: Read all contents of a file from LittleFS as a String.
fs.existsTooltip: Check if a file exists in LittleFS. Returns true if found.

ticker.attachTooltip: Execute handler code periodically at the specified interval (ESP32 only, uses Ticker.h)
ticker.checkTooltip: Check and execute the ticker handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.

blocks.math_maptooltip: Re-maps a number from one range to another (Arduino map function)

sensor.ultrasonic_distancetooltip: Measure distance with the ultrasonic sensor (cm).
sensor.dht_inittooltip: Initialize a DHT temperature & humidity sensor.
sensor.dht_temperaturetooltip: Read the temperature from the DHT sensor (°C).
sensor.dht_humiditytooltip: Read the humidity from the DHT sensor (%).
sensor.rus04_rgbtooltip: Set colors on both eyes of RUS-04.
sensor.rus04_bothsimpletooltip: Set both eyes' color on RUS-04.
sensor.rus04_offtooltip: Turn off all RGB LEDs on RUS-04.
sensor.wall_errortooltip: Get wall error for PID control

audio.buzzerToneTooltip: Play a tone on the buzzer

setup.label: Setup
setup.tooltip: Runs once when the program starts

loop.label: Loop
loop.tooltip: Runs repeatedly while the program is running

digitalWrite.tooltip: Write HIGH(1) or LOW(0) to digital pin

millis.label: Elapsed time (ms)
millis.tooltip: Returns elapsed time since program start in milliseconds

serialBegin.label: Serial begin
serialBegin.tooltip: Start serial communication at specified baud rate

serialPrint.label: Serial print
serialPrint.tooltip: Output value to serial monitor (no newline)

serialPrintln.label: Serial println
serialPrintln.tooltip: Output value to serial monitor with newline

esp32.touch_attach_tooltip: Executes HANDLER when the ESP32 touch pin drops below the threshold (= touched). Uses touchAttachInterrupt ISR + loopPre_ for safe loop-context execution. HANDLER must be placed inside arduino_loop.
esp32.touch_read_tooltip: Reads the raw value of an ESP32 capacitive touch pin (0-1023; high = untouched, low = touched). Works only on original ESP32 + S2/S3, not on C3/C6/H2.

stepper.moveTooltip: Move motor by specified number of steps
stepper.stopTooltip: Stop motor and release coil current

motor.moveTooltip: Move motor in specified direction and speed (0-255)

servo.sweepTooltip: Move servo from start angle to end angle

neopixel.allledcolortooltip: Set all LEDs to specified color

diffDrive.initTooltip: Initialize differential drive robot. Track width is distance between wheels.
diffDrive.setSpeedTooltip: Set left/right motor speeds independently (-255 to 255)
diffDrive.spinTooltip: Rotate left or right in place
diffDrive.lineTraceTooltip: Line trace driving with PID correction

pid.initTooltip: Initialize PID controller. Kp=proportional, Ki=integral, Kd=derivative gain
pid.name: Name
pid.resetTooltip: Reset PID integral and derivative values
pid.motorSpeedCalcTooltip: Calculate left/right motor speeds with PID and store in global variables

http.getTooltip: Send a GET request to the specified URL and get the response
http.getWithHeadersTooltip: Send a GET request with custom headers (for API Key auth, etc.)
http.postTooltip: Send a POST request to the specified URL and get the response
http.putTooltip: Send a PUT request to the specified URL
http.deleteTooltip: Send a DELETE request to the specified URL (returns HTTP status code)
http.isSuccessTooltip: Check if the HTTP status code is in the 200 range
http.buildUrlTooltip: Add query parameters to a base URL

json.getStringTooltip: Get a string value from JSON by key
json.getNumberTooltip: Get a number value from JSON by key
json.getIntTooltip: Get an integer value from JSON by key
json.getBoolTooltip: Get a boolean value from JSON by key
json.getNestedTooltip: Get a value from nested JSON (e.g., data.items[0].name)
json.hasKeyTooltip: Check if the key exists in JSON
json.arraySizeTooltip: Get the size of a JSON array
json.arrayGetTooltip: Get an element from a JSON array at the specified index
json.setStringTooltip: Set a string value in JSON
json.setNumberTooltip: Set a number value in JSON
json.setBoolTooltip: Set a boolean value in JSON
json.toStringTooltip: Convert the created JSON object to a string
json.toStringPrettyTooltip: Convert the created JSON object to a formatted string
json.createtooltip: Start creating a new JSON object
json.parsewithsizetooltip: Increase buffer size for large JSON

mqtt.setupTooltip: Configure WiFi and MQTT broker connection
mqtt.connectTooltip: Connect to MQTT broker (with or without auth)
mqtt.publishTooltip: Publish a message to the specified topic
mqtt.subscribeTooltip: Subscribe to the specified topic (wildcards # and + supported)
mqtt.onMessageTooltip: Execute when MQTT message is received. mqtt_topic and mqtt_message variables are available.
mqtt.loopTooltip: Process MQTT communication. Call in loop().
mqtt.isConnectedTooltip: Check if connected to MQTT broker
mqtt.lastWillTooltip: Set the message the broker sends when client disconnects unexpectedly
mqtt.connectWithLwtTooltip: Connect to MQTT broker including Last Will settings
mqtt.publishQosTooltip: Publish a message with specified QoS level
mqtt.buffersizetooltip: Set maximum MQTT message size (default 256 bytes)
mqtt.keepalivetooltip: Set MQTT keep alive interval (default 15 seconds)
mqtt.receivedmessagetooltip: Get the content of received message
mqtt.receivedtopictooltip: Get the topic name of received message

wifi.connectTooltip: Connect to a WiFi network using SSID and password
wifi.getIpTooltip: Get the current IP address as a string
wifi.getRssiTooltip: Get WiFi signal strength in dBm (-30:very strong ~ -90:very weak)

ota.setupSimpleTooltip: Enable OTA when already connected to WiFi (use after mqtt_setup or ha_device_init)
ota.loopTooltip: Check for OTA updates. Call in loop().

esp.deepSleepTooltip: Enter deep sleep for the specified seconds (power saving mode)
esp.lightSleepTooltip: Enter light sleep for the specified seconds (resumes after)
esp.getFreeHeapTooltip: Get free heap memory in bytes
esp.getChipIdTooltip: Get the unique ESP32 chip ID
esp.getCpuFreqTooltip: Get the CPU frequency in MHz
esp.getFlashSizeTooltip: Get the flash memory size in bytes
esp.getSketchSizeTooltip: Get the current sketch size in bytes
esp.getFreeSketchSpaceTooltip: Get the free sketch space for OTA in bytes

watchdog.enableTooltip: Enable ESP32 task watchdog timer (TWDT). If loop() blocks longer than the timeout, the ESP32 panics and auto-restarts. Place inside arduino_setup. Set the timeout above your longest expected loop iteration; long blocking calls (HTTP, OTA download) should be split or call esp_task_wdt_reset() manually.

ha.deviceInitTooltip: Initialize a device that auto-registers with Home Assistant. Manufacturer / Model / Software Version appear on the HA Devices page. Auto deviceId (MAC) avoids collisions across multiple ESP32. Availability (LWT) marks the device Unavailable in HA UI on disconnect.
ha.deviceInitAuthTooltip: Initialize HA device with MQTT authentication. Manufacturer / Model / Software Version appear on the HA Devices page. Auto deviceId (MAC) avoids collisions across multiple ESP32. Availability (LWT) marks the device Unavailable in HA UI on disconnect.
ha.sensorCreateTooltip: Create a sensor to register with Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language). Icon overrides the HA UI icon.
ha.sensorUpdateTooltip: Send sensor value to Home Assistant
ha.binarySensorCreateTooltip: Create an ON/OFF binary sensor. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language). Icon overrides the HA UI icon.
ha.binarySensorUpdateTooltip: Send binary sensor state to Home Assistant
ha.switchCreateTooltip: Create a switch controllable from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.switchOnCommandTooltip: Execute when switch command is received from Home Assistant
ha.switchSetStateTooltip: Send switch state to Home Assistant
ha.lightCreateTooltip: Create a light controllable from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.lightOnCommandTooltip: Execute when light command is received. ha_light_state and ha_light_brightness are available.
ha.lightStateTooltip: Get the received light ON/OFF state
ha.lightSetStateTooltip: Report light state to Home Assistant
ha.buttonCreateTooltip: Create a button pressable from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.buttonOnPressTooltip: Execute when button is pressed from Home Assistant
ha.loopTooltip: Maintain communication with Home Assistant and auto-reconnect WiFi every 5 s if disconnected. Call inside loop().
ha.isConnectedTooltip: Check if connected to Home Assistant
ha.numberCreateTooltip: Create an entity that accepts number input from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.numberOnCommandTooltip: Execute when number is changed from Home Assistant. ha_number_value is available.
ha.numberSetStateTooltip: Report number state to Home Assistant
ha.fanCreateTooltip: Create a fan controllable from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.fanOnCommandTooltip: Execute when fan command is received. ha_fan_state and ha_fan_speed are available.
ha.fanStateTooltip: Get the received fan ON/OFF state
ha.fanSetStateTooltip: Report fan state to Home Assistant
ha.coverCreateTooltip: Create a cover (shutter, etc.) controllable from Home Assistant. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.coverOnCommandTooltip: Execute when cover command is received from Home Assistant
ha.coverSetStateTooltip: Report cover state to Home Assistant
ha.lightCreateRgbTooltip: Create an RGB color-controllable light. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.lightOnRgbCommandTooltip: Execute when RGB color is changed from Home Assistant. ha_light_r/g/b are available.
ha.lightSetRgbTooltip: Report RGB color to Home Assistant
ha.triggerCreateTooltip: Register an event that can be used as a HA automation trigger
ha.sceneCreateTooltip: Create a scene executable from HA. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.sceneOnCommandTooltip: Execute when scene is triggered from Home Assistant
ha.tagScannerCreateTooltip: Register NFC tag scanner, etc. Object ID = entity_id (ASCII snake_case). Display Name = HA UI label (any language).
ha.reportIntervalTooltip: Execute HA reporting at specified intervals (use in loop)
ha.diagnosticsAutoTooltip: Automatically publish RSSI / Uptime / Free Heap / Reset Reason to Home Assistant at the specified interval (default 60 s). Operates on an independent millis() timer (separate from ha_report_interval). entity_category=diagnostic is applied via the ha_loop retain-override path so the entities appear under the Diagnostics section of the HA UI. Place inside arduino_setup; at least one item must be enabled.
ha.onConnectedTooltip: Fires when MQTT connection to the HA broker is acquired (initial connect + every reconnect). Place once per project — HAMqtt holds a single callback, so multiple placements silently overwrite each other.
ha.onDisconnectedTooltip: Fires when MQTT connection to the HA broker is lost. Place once per project — HAMqtt holds a single callback, so multiple placements silently overwrite each other.
ha.otaSetupTooltip: Register a Home Assistant Update entity so the ESP32 fetches new firmware itself when you click Install in HA. Object ID is the HA entity_id (ASCII), Display Name is the HA UI label, Firmware URL is the .bin location reachable from the ESP32 (HA add-on local/<file>.bin or any HTTP server). Place inside arduino_setup, after ha_device_init. The software version is shared from the ha_device_init field. Workflow: build in DigiCode → click Download .bin in the compile dialog → upload .bin to the URL → click Install in HA UI. Auto-distribution / signature verification are polish-phase work.
ha.tagscannedtooltip: Report scanned tag ID to HA

init.label: Humanoid Init
init.tooltip: Initialize Humanoid robot (pin numbers from preset)

gesture.label: Humanoid Gesture
gesture.tooltip: Humanoid expresses emotions with movement and sound

mode.label: Ninja Mode
mode.tooltip: Switch between Walk (4-legged) and Roll (wheel) modes

a4988.direction_tooltip: Set the A4988 direction. Next a4988_step runs forward (positive) or backward (negative).
a4988.init_tooltip: Initialize A4988 stepper driver. Specify STEP/DIR/EN pins. EN active LOW. Uses AccelStepper DRIVER mode.
a4988.microstep_tooltip: Set A4988 microstep mode (internal step multiplier). ⚠️ The MS1/MS2/MS3 pins on the HW must be wired to match this value.
a4988.step_tooltip: Run A4988 by the specified number of steps (microstep factor included). Blocks until completion via runToNewPosition.

array.createtooltip: Create an array. Specify type, dimension, size or content.
array.gettooltip: Get value at specified position in array.
array.settooltip: Set value at specified position in array.
array.sizetooltip: Get the number of elements in the array.

azure.iot_central_connect_tooltip: Connect to Azure IoT Central. Scope ID / Device ID / Device Key can be obtained from the IoT Central device page. Internally registers with IoT Hub via DPS. Requires WiFi connection.
azure.iot_central_publish_tooltip: Publishes telemetry to Azure IoT Central as a key/value pair (internally converted to JSON). Requires prior connection.
azure.iot_hub_connect_tooltip: Connect to Azure IoT Hub via connection string. Requires WiFi. Returns true on success.
azure.iot_hub_on_c2d: On Azure IoT Hub message received
azure.iot_hub_on_c2d_tooltip: Defines the handler executed when a Cloud-to-Device (C2D) message arrives from Azure IoT Hub. Use the "received message" block inside HANDLER to access the value. Place inside arduino_loop.
azure.iot_hub_publish_d2c: Send telemetry to Azure IoT Hub
azure.iot_hub_publish_d2c_tooltip: Send a Device-to-Cloud (D2C) message to Azure IoT Hub. Requires prior connection. Typically a JSON string is passed.
azure.iot_is_connected_tooltip: Returns true while the MQTT connection to Azure IoT Hub / IoT Central is alive. Becomes false on WiFi disconnect or TLS timeout.
azure.iot_received_value_tooltip: Retrieves the received message inside an Azure IoT Hub C2D handler. Returns empty string outside HANDLER.
azure.iot_subscribe_direct_method_tooltip: Defines the handler executed when a Direct Method call is received via Azure IoT. The payload (JSON) is accessible via the "received message" block inside HANDLER. Place inside arduino_loop.
azure.iot_update_device_twin_tooltip: Update Azure IoT Device Twin reported properties. Requires prior connection.

dfp.inittooltip: Initialize DFPlayer Mini via SoftwareSerial. Returns true if found. Connect DFPlayer TX to ESP32 RX pin, and DFPlayer RX (via 1kΩ resistor) to ESP32 TX pin.
dfp.playtooltip: Play the specified track number (1-999) from the SD card.

epaper.clear_tooltip: Clears the E-paper with white (requires full_refresh or partial_update to apply).
epaper.draw_image_tooltip: Draws a PROGMEM bitmap on E-paper. The image variable name must be defined as const unsigned char beforehand (raw cpp edit required).
epaper.full_tooltip: Performs a full-screen refresh of the E-paper (slow, heavy flicker, removes ghosting). Recommended once a day or every few hours.
epaper.partial_tooltip: Partially updates the E-paper (fast, low flicker, ghosting may remain over long use).
epaper.print_tooltip: Prints a string on E-paper at (X, Y). Requires epaper_partial_update or epaper_full_refresh to display.

google.sheets_append_tooltip: POSTs JSON to a Google Apps Script Webhook URL to append a row to a Google Sheet. Requires WiFi. Implement doPost(e) in Apps Script and call Sheet.appendRow().
google.sheets_format_row_tooltip: Combines timestamp and value into JSON format ({

gps.get_altitude_tooltip: Returns the GPS altitude (m above sea level). 0.0 when not yet fixed.
gps.get_lat_tooltip: Returns GPS latitude (degrees; + north / − south). Includes location.isValid() check; returns 0.0 when not fixed.
gps.get_lng_tooltip: Returns GPS longitude (degrees; + east / − west). 0.0 when not yet fixed.
gps.get_satellites_tooltip: Returns the number of GPS satellites in view. 3+ allows 2D fix; 4+ allows 3D fix.
gps.init_tooltip: Initialize the GPS NEO-6M/8M module. UART via HardwareSerial2 (default 9600 baud). loopPre_ does continuous NMEA parsing. Outdoor cold start may take 30 seconds to several minutes.

lora.init_tooltip: Initialize the LoRa SX1276/77/78/79 transceiver. SPI-based, specifying SS (CS), RST and DIO0 (RX interrupt) pins. Default 915 MHz.
lora.on_receive_tooltip: Executes HANDLER when a LoRa packet is received. loopPre_ injects a tick; HANDLER must be placed inside arduino_loop.
lora.received_value_tooltip: Returns the LoRa-received packet data. Valid only inside the lora_on_receive HANDLER (empty string outside HANDLER).
lora.send_tooltip: Send a LoRa packet. Requires lora_init beforehand.
lora.set_freq_tooltip: Set LoRa frequency. ⚠️ Choose per regional regulations (Japan = 920 MHz, EU = 868, Americas = 915).
lora.set_power_tooltip: Set LoRa TX power (2-20 dBm, uses PA_BOOST pin). ⚠️ Subject to regional power limits (Japan = 13 dBm upper limit).

max30102.heart_rate_tooltip: Returns heart rate (BPM) from MAX30102 (4-beat average). ⚠️ Takes 5–10 s to stabilize; not medical-grade.
max30102.init_tooltip: Initialize MAX30102 heart-rate/SpO2 sensor (I2C 0x57). ⚠️ Educational use, not medical-grade. Press a finger lightly to use.
max30102.spo2_tooltip: Returns a simplified SpO2 (%) from MAX30102 (Red/IR ratio based). ⚠️ Educational use, not medical-grade; 10+ s needed to stabilize.

mic.i2s_init_tooltip: Initialize an I2S MEMS microphone (INMP441 etc.). Specify BCLK / LRCK (WS) / DIN (DOUT) pins. 16 kHz / 24-bit / left channel only.
mic.read_amplitude_tooltip: Reads 512 samples from the I2S microphone and returns RMS amplitude (24-bit normalized value).
mic.read_db_tooltip: Returns relative dB from the I2S microphone (20*log10(RMS)). ⚠️ Absolute dB SPL requires calibration with an external reference sound source.

modbus.init_tooltip: Initialize a Modbus RTU master. Uses UART2 + DE/RE pin via an RS485 transceiver (MAX485 etc.). Defaults to slave ID = 1; change with modbus_set_slave_id.
modbus.read_holding_tooltip: Reads a single Modbus holding register (function code 0x03). Returns -1 on failure.
modbus.set_slave_id_tooltip: Sets the Modbus slave ID (1-247). When talking to multiple slaves, switch before each communication.
modbus.write_register_tooltip: Writes a value to a Modbus register (function code 0x06).

oled.ssd1306_clear_tooltip: Clears the OLED frame buffer. Call oled_ssd1306_display to push to the real screen.
oled.ssd1306_display_tooltip: Pushes the OLED frame buffer to the real screen. Must be called after clear/print/draw_*.
oled.ssd1306_draw_circle_tooltip: Draws a circle on the OLED (X,Y = center, R = radius).
oled.ssd1306_draw_line_tooltip: Draws a straight line from (X1,Y1) to (X2,Y2) on the OLED.
oled.ssd1306_draw_pixel_tooltip: Draws a 1-pixel point at (X,Y) on the OLED.
oled.ssd1306_draw_rect_tooltip: Draws a rectangle on the OLED (X,Y = top-left, W = width, H = height).
oled.ssd1306_init_tooltip: Initialize an SSD1306 OLED display over I2C. Typical default is 128x64 / 0x3C. Uses the Adafruit SSD1306 lib.
oled.ssd1306_print_tooltip: Prints text from the specified (X,Y). Draws into the frame buffer; call oled_ssd1306_display to push.
oled.ssd1306_set_text_size_tooltip: Sets the OLED text size (1 = 6x8 px, 2 = 12x16 px, ...)

pms5003.init_tooltip: Initialize the PMS5003 PM2.5/PM10 dust sensor (UART 9600 baud). Call read() each second to refresh data.
pms5003.read_pm10_tooltip: Returns PM10 (particles ≤ 10 μm) concentration. WHO 24h guideline: 45 μg/m³.
pms5003.read_pm25_tooltip: Returns PM2.5 (particles ≤ 2.5 μm) concentration. WHO 24h guideline: 15 μg/m³; Japan environmental standard: 35 μg/m³.

relay.init_tooltip: Initialize the relay. Active polarity = output level when ON (mechanical typically HIGH, SSR typically LOW).
relay.off_tooltip: Turns the relay OFF (inverse polarity of active). Requires relay_init beforehand.
relay.on_tooltip: Turns the relay ON (follows active polarity). Requires relay_init beforehand.
relay.toggle_tooltip: Toggles the relay state (outputs the inverse of digitalRead).

scd30.init_tooltip: Initialize the SCD30 NDIR CO2 + temp/humidity sensor (I2C 0x61, Sensirion official lib). startPeriodicMeasurement begins 2-second-interval measurements.
scd30.read_co2_tooltip: Returns SCD30 CO2 concentration in ppm (range 400-10000; typical outdoor 420, indoor 600-1500, alarm 1000+).
scd30.read_temp_tooltip: Returns SCD30 temperature in ℃. NDIR self-heating is compensated internally.

acs712.init_tooltip: Initialize ACS712 Hall-effect current sensor. Supports 5A/20A/30A models (identified by mV/A sensitivity). Uses robtillaart/ACS712 lib with built-in RMS calculation.
acs712.read_current_tooltip: Returns ACS712 current in A (mA → A conversion, DC measurement). Requires acs712_init beforehand.

ac.current_clamp_calibrate_tooltip: Measures the AC clamp current sensor zero point (~100 ms). Run with the load disconnected.
ac.current_clamp_init_tooltip: Initialize AC clamp current sensor (SCT-013 etc.). Specify pin (ADC), winding ratio (Current Transformer ratio), and burden resistor (Ω).
ac.current_clamp_read_rms_tooltip: Returns the RMS value of AC clamp current (A). About 100 samples cover at least one 50/60Hz cycle. Requires ac_current_clamp_init beforehand.

apds9960.init_tooltip: Initialize APDS9960 (gesture/color/proximity) over I2C (0x39). Enables proximity + gesture.
apds9960.read_proximity_tooltip: Returns APDS9960 proximity value (0-255, larger = closer).

env3.init_tooltip: Initialize M5Stack ENV III unit (SHT30 temp/humidity + QMP6988 pressure) over I2C. Standard device for the FS course IoT thermometer.
env3.read_humidity_tooltip: Read humidity (%) from ENV III SHT30. Requires env3_init beforehand.
env3.read_pressure_tooltip: Read pressure (hPa) from ENV III QMP6988. Requires env3_init beforehand.
env3.read_temperature_tooltip: Read temperature (°C) from ENV III SHT30. Requires env3_init beforehand.

env4.init_tooltip: Initialize M5Stack ENV IV unit (SHT40 temp/humidity + BMP280 pressure) over I2C. Read values via sht40_read_temperature / sht40_read_humidity / bmp280_read.

espnow.broadcast_tooltip: Broadcast to all ESP-NOW devices (FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF). Requires prior espnow_init.
espnow.get_my_mac_tooltip: Get the device's own MAC address (
espnow.init_tooltip: Initialize ESP-NOW. Sets WiFi STA mode, calls esp_now_init, and registers the receive callback. ESP32 family only.
espnow.on_receive_tooltip: Defines the handler executed when an ESP-NOW message is received. Inside HANDLER, retrieve values via espnow_received_data / espnow_received_mac. Place inside arduino_loop.
espnow.received_data_tooltip: Retrieves received data inside an ESP-NOW receive handler. Returns empty string outside HANDLER.
espnow.received_mac_tooltip: Retrieves the source MAC inside an ESP-NOW receive handler (
espnow.register_peer_tooltip: Pre-register an ESP-NOW peer. The MAC address is
espnow.send_tooltip: Unicast to an ESP-NOW peer at the specified MAC. Auto-adds peer if not registered. Requires prior espnow_init.

flow.meter_get_rate_tooltip: Returns flow meter rate in L/min (updated every 1 second; returns previous value in between). Requires flow_meter_init beforehand.
flow.meter_init_tooltip: Initialize the YF-S201 flow meter. Connect to an interrupt pin (RISING edge), uses internal INPUT_PULLUP, F=7.5*Q conversion (per datasheet).

hx711.calibrate_tooltip: Calibrate HX711 with a known weight. Run tare() first to zero, place the known weight, then call this block. The built-in calibrate_scale function computes the scale factor automatically.
hx711.init_tooltip: Initialize HX711 load-cell ADC. Specify DOUT (data out) and SCK (clock) pins. Uses robtillaart/HX711 lib.
hx711.read_raw_tooltip: Reads the raw ADC value from HX711 (offset/scale not applied). Requires hx711_init beforehand.
hx711.read_weight_tooltip: Reads weight from HX711 (g, averaged over 10 samples). Requires hx711_init + hx711_calibrate (or set_scale) beforehand.
hx711.set_scale_tooltip: Manually sets HX711 scale factor. Use when bypassing calibrate_scale and specifying a known factor (e.g., 420.5).
hx711.tare_tooltip: Resets HX711 zero to the current value (tare, averaged over 10 samples). Call with no load.

ina219.init_tooltip: Initialize INA219 high-precision current/voltage/power sensor (I2C). Default address 0x40. Uses robtillaart/INA219 lib.
ina219.read_current_tooltip: Returns INA219 current in mA. Requires ina219_init beforehand.
ina219.read_power_tooltip: Returns INA219 power in mW. Requires ina219_init beforehand.
ina219.read_voltage_tooltip: Returns INA219 bus voltage in V. Requires ina219_init beforehand.

iot.cloud_connect_tooltip: Connect to IoT cloud. Pick a provider and pass JSON credentials (e.g., Azure Hub: connection_string; Central: scope_id+device_id+device_key; MQTT: broker+port). AWS / GCP are planned for Phase D (currently stubs).
iot.cloud_disconnect_tooltip: Disconnect from the IoT cloud. Resets the provider state.
iot.cloud_is_connected_tooltip: Returns true while the IoT cloud connection is alive. Reflects Azure / MQTT client state depending on the provider.
iot.cloud_on_message_tooltip: Defines the handler executed when a message arrives from the IoT cloud. Inside HANDLER, retrieve the value with iot_cloud_received_value. Place inside arduino_loop.
iot.cloud_publish_tooltip: Publish to the IoT cloud. Requires iot_cloud_connect first. Dispatches to Azure D2C / generic MQTT etc. depending on the provider.
iot.cloud_received_value_tooltip: Retrieves the received value inside an IoT cloud message handler. Returns empty string outside HANDLER.

m5stack.battery_level_tooltip: Get M5Stack built-in battery level as 0-100 % (M5.Power.getBatteryLevel).
m5stack.battery_voltage_tooltip: Get M5Stack built-in battery voltage in V (M5.Power.getBatteryVoltage returns mV; this block divides by 1000).
m5stack.begin_tooltip: Initialize the M5Stack board core (LCD/buttons/speaker/battery). Uses the M5Unified lib, with a 9-model common API. Place inside arduino_setup.
m5stack.button_a_held_tooltip: Returns true while button A has been held for ≥ 1 second (pressedFor(1000)). Requires prior m5stack_begin + m5stack_update.
m5stack.button_a_pressed_tooltip: Returns true if button A was pressed within this loop iteration (wasPressed). Requires prior m5stack_begin + m5stack_update at the top of loop.
m5stack.button_b_held_tooltip: Returns true while button B has been held for ≥ 1 second.
m5stack.button_b_pressed_tooltip: Returns true if button B was pressed within this loop iteration.
m5stack.button_c_held_tooltip: Returns true while button C has been held for ≥ 1 second.
m5stack.button_c_pressed_tooltip: Returns true if button C was pressed within this loop iteration.
m5stack.lcd_clear_tooltip: Fills the M5Stack LCD with black and returns the cursor to (0,0).
m5stack.lcd_print_at_tooltip: Prints text on the M5Stack LCD at the specified (X,Y) coordinates. setCursor then print.
m5stack.lcd_print_tooltip: Prints text on the M5Stack built-in LCD (from the current cursor, with newline).
m5stack.lcd_set_text_size_tooltip: Sets the M5Stack LCD text-size multiplier (1 = default, 2 = 2x etc).
m5stack.speaker_tone_tooltip: Plays a tone at the specified frequency for the specified duration on the M5Stack built-in speaker (M5.Speaker.tone).
m5stack.update_tooltip: Updates the M5Stack button/touch state. Must be called at the top of loop (otherwise press detection stops working).

max7219.clear_tooltip: Turns off all MAX7219 matrix LEDs (buffer reset + immediate refresh).
max7219.init_tooltip: Initialize an MAX7219 8x8 LED matrix. Daisy-chain up to N modules (max 8). SPI-based, inline driver.
max7219.scroll_text_tooltip: Scrolls a string left on the MAX7219 matrix (5x7 font, ASCII 0x20-0x5A). Speed (ms) is the per-column delay.
max7219.set_brightness_tooltip: Sets the MAX7219 matrix brightness from 0 (darkest) to 15 (brightest).
max7219.set_pixel_tooltip: Sets a specific pixel ON/OFF on the MAX7219 matrix. Writes to buffer; call show to apply.
max7219.show_tooltip: Pushes the MAX7219 buffer contents to the physical LEDs. Required after set_pixel.

neomatrix.clear_tooltip: Turns off all NeoMatrix LEDs (buffer reset; call show to apply).
neomatrix.init_tooltip: Initialize NeoMatrix (NeoPixel 2D). Default 8x8, TOP+LEFT+ROWS+PROGRESSIVE wiring. WS2812B / SK6812 compatible.
neomatrix.set_pixel_tooltip: Sets a NeoMatrix pixel color. The color is a hex string
neomatrix.show_tooltip: Pushes the NeoMatrix buffer to the physical LEDs. Required after set_pixel.

piezo.read_vibration_tooltip: Reads piezo (PZT) vibration via ADC (0-4095, ESP32 12-bit). Larger = stronger vibration.
piezo.threshold_tooltip: Returns true when piezo vibration exceeds the specified threshold (typical threshold = 1000; tune as needed).

pushover.send_tooltip: Sends a Pushover notification. The App token (from app creation) and User key (from account top page) are obtained at pushover.net. Requires WiFi. Free tier allows 10000 msg/month.

qmp6988.init_tooltip: Initialize the QMP6988 pressure sensor (built into M5 ENV III / stand-alone) over I2C. Uses M5Unit-ENV lib.
qmp6988.read_pressure_tooltip: Reads pressure (hPa) from QMP6988. Requires qmp6988_init beforehand.

sht30.init_tooltip: Initialize SHT30 temp/humidity sensor (Sensirion) at I2C address 0x44. Communicates via Wire (SDA/SCL pins board-default).
sht30.read_humidity_tooltip: Reads humidity (%) from SHT30. Requires sht30_init beforehand. Returns 0 on error.
sht30.read_temperature_tooltip: Reads temperature (°C) from SHT30. Requires sht30_init beforehand. Returns 0 on error.

sht40.init_tooltip: Initialize SHT40 temp/humidity sensor (Sensirion successor, via Adafruit) at I2C address 0x44. Communicates via Wire (SDA/SCL pins board-default).
sht40.read_humidity_tooltip: Reads humidity (%) from SHT40. Requires sht40_init beforehand. Returns 0 on error.
sht40.read_temperature_tooltip: Reads temperature (°C) from SHT40. Requires sht40_init beforehand. Returns 0 on error.

tm1637.clear_tooltip: Turns off all digits on TM1637.
tm1637.init_tooltip: Initialize a TM1637 4-digit 7-segment display. Uses akj7/TM1637 Driver lib, fixed 4 digits (TOTAL_DIGITS=4); colon is controlled via colonOn()/colonOff().
tm1637.show_number_tooltip: Displays an integer on TM1637 (-999 to 9999, colon off). Requires tm1637_init beforehand.
tm1637.show_time_tooltip: Displays the time in HH:MM format on TM1637 (colon on). Requires tm1637_init beforehand.

uln2003.init_tooltip: Initialize ULN2003 stepper driver + 28BYJ-48. AccelStepper FULL4WIRE mode (pin order IN1, IN3, IN2, IN4 for normal rotation).
uln2003.step_tooltip: Runs ULN2003 + 28BYJ-48 by the specified steps and speed (28BYJ-48 1 revolution = 4096 steps in half-step).

localServerSetup.installDescription: Starts the compile server locally with Docker. Run the command below in your Terminal or PowerShell.
localServerSetup.uninstallDescription: Removes the container and persistent volumes (including the compile cache). Run the command below in your Terminal or PowerShell.
localServerSetup.windowsHint: Open "Windows PowerShell" (64-bit; not the "x86" or "ISE" variants). On Windows 11 with PowerShell 7 installed, plain "PowerShell" also works. No admin privileges required.
localServerSetup.installNoteDocker: Docker is required. If not installed, the script prints OS-specific download URLs.
localServerSetup.installNotePort: The script asks which host port to use on first run. Press Enter to accept 3001 if it's free; if 3001 is in use, an alternate port (e.g., 3002) is suggested.
localServerSetup.uninstallWarning: Removing the container and volumes also deletes the compile cache. The next compile will be a cold start (~30–60s). The Docker image can be kept on request.
localServerSetup.fullGuide: Open the full guide in a new tab
localServerSetup.guiStep1: Launch Docker Desktop (if not installed, download from the OS-specific link below)
localServerSetup.guiStep2: Type the following image name into the top search bar → Pull
localServerSetup.guiStep3: On the Images tab, click "Run" on the image → expand Optional settings
localServerSetup.guiStep4: Container name (e.g., digicode-compile-server), Host port: 3001, Container port: 3001 → Run
localServerSetup.guiStep5: Open the URL below in a browser to verify (OK = success)
localServerSetup.guiNotePort: If port 3001 is taken, pick another. Enter the same number under DigiCode's "Compile settings" → Local server.
localServerSetup.guiUninstall1: Open the "Containers" tab in Docker Desktop
localServerSetup.guiUninstall2: Stop and Delete the digicode-compile-server container
localServerSetup.guiUninstall3: On the "Volumes" tab, Delete digicode-projects / digicode-cache (the cache will be erased)
localServerSetup.guiUninstall4: On the "Images" tab, Delete digicollc/digicode-compile-server (optional; keep it to make re-install faster)
localServerSetup.aiDescription: Copy the prompt below and paste it into the AI assistant you use — Gemini / ChatGPT / Claude etc. Each prompt asks the AI to look up the latest Docker Desktop screen layout first, so the answer reflects the current UI.
localServerSetup.aiPullRunHint: Use this prompt when you haven't set up the local server yet, or when the container isn't running.
localServerSetup.aiVerifyHint: Use this prompt when the server is set up but the connection test fails.
localServerSetup.aiPullRunPrompt:
Tell me how, in the latest Docker Desktop UI, to search for an image on DockerHub, pull it, and run a container.
Please look up the current Docker Desktop screen layout first, then answer.
- Image name: digicollc/digicode-compile-server
- Container name: digicode-compile-server
- Host port: 3001
Walk me through the setup with these values, step by step for a beginner.
localServerSetup.aiVerifyPrompt:
Tell me how to verify a container is running properly in Docker Desktop.
Please look up the current Docker Desktop screen layout first, then answer.
The container name is digicode-compile-server and the port is 3001.
Include how to check via http://localhost:3001/health in a browser.

intro.heading: What is a Local LLM
intro.point1: AI models that run on your own PC. No API fees, works offline.
intro.point2: Usable with DigiCode AI block generation, help bot, and UI customization.

appleSi.ram: 32 GB+ unified memory (M3 Pro/Max or higher)

notes.heading: Notes
notes.env: Actual behavior in DigiCode varies by environment and model.
notes.quality: Block generation quality depends on model capability. We recommend 14B+ models for complex compositions.
notes.oldPc: A PC from 2020 or later is recommended. For older PCs, prefer cloud APIs (Gemini / GPT / Claude).
notes.fallback: If you run into issues, fall back to a cloud API.
notes.disclaimer: The tools and models listed here are examples of local LLM setups, not endorsements or compatibility guarantees for DigiCode.

apiKeys.title: How to get an API key

intro.heading: What is an API key?
intro.point1: An API key is the credential that lets you access each AI provider's (Google / OpenAI / Anthropic) developer service.
intro.point2: These developer services are separate from monthly subscriptions (ChatGPT Plus / Claude Pro / Gemini Advanced) and billed separately. Holding a subscription does not include API usage.
intro.point3: API usage is metered (pay-as-you-go). For everyday DigiCode block generation, a few thousand yen of credit typically lasts months to years (see each provider's pricing page for details).

providers.heading: How to obtain a key, per provider

gemini.name: Gemini (Google)
gemini.steps: Sign in to Google AI Studio → side menu "API keys" → "Create API key" → select a project → copy the generated key.

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.steps: Sign in to OpenAI Platform → account menu (top right) → "API keys" → "Create new secret key" → copy the generated key (only shown once).

anthropic.name: Claude (Anthropic)
anthropic.steps: Sign in to Anthropic Console → side menu "API Keys" → "Create Key" → enter a key name → copy the generated key.

alternative.heading: If you'd rather not pay
alternative.body: You can use Local LLM, which requires no API billing. AI models run on your own PC and your data is never sent externally.

notes.heading: Notes
notes.localStorage: API keys are stored in your browser's local storage as plain text. We recommend deleting them after use on shared computers.
notes.byok: DigiCode does not transmit your API keys to our servers. AI calls go directly from your browser to each provider (BYOK).
notes.pricing: Provider pricing may change at any time. Please refer to each provider's official pricing page for current rates.
